access.anon: Cannot call MCP tools that require user context.
access.user: After authorization, can call personal workspace-related tools.
access.admin: Currently does not automatically gain additional MCP admin tools due to admin status.
access.agent: MCP is one of the protocol entry points alongside REST, covering capabilities aligned with the web by default while enforcing resource-bound bearer tokens with canonical feature:action scopes.

rules.personal-workspace-focus: MCP focuses by default on personal learning workspace, public query, and low-risk personal state write capabilities; admin capabilities are not exposed by default.
rules.text-formatted-json: Every tool result is one object with a required success boolean. The text JSON and structuredContent representations are identical; non-object domain payloads are nested under result.
rules.output-modes: MCP has one canonical compact response structure in default mode and an opt-in full mode that adds raw fields without changing top-level collection types. The legacy summary input remains accepted as a deprecated alias for default and never introduces summary wrapper objects.
rules.coverage: The checked tool list below is the MCP contract surface; implementation parity is enforced through the checks in $life-ustc-dev-loop.
rules.aggregate-before-fanout: Prefer assistant-oriented aggregate or filtered tools first; raw dataset tools remain available for power clients that need local post-processing.
rules.privacy-safe-default: Default output may omit repeated low-value nested objects and redact token-bearing URLs or other sensitive strings; full mode is the explicit escape hatch when exact raw values are required.
rules.property-priority-parity: MCP default output follows the shared UI Model Property Priority contract: keep primary identity/task fields and useful disambiguators, compact repeated nested objects, and omit low-priority raw/audit fields unless full mode is explicitly requested.
rules.actionable-errors: Validation and common not-found payloads prefer plain-language messages and may include a hint that points to the next useful tool or query to recover.
rules.resource-bound-access-token: MCP transport requests must present a resource-bound Bearer token for /api/mcp. JWT access tokens minted with the canonical MCP resource are accepted; opaque tokens without a resource indicator are rejected. Omitted-resource refresh grants are normalized to MCP only when the stored grant includes MCP-compatible feature scopes and the canonical MCP resource approval.
rules.complete-batch-scope-enforcement: Every fixed-scope tool feature declared by each requested tool is required across the complete JSON-RPC batch. A feature:write grant also satisfies feature:read for the same feature, while the legacy mcp:tools scope retains compatibility access. The dynamic registered GraphQL runner applies the selected operation's exact scopes immediately before execution.
rules.registered-graphql-authorization: run_graphql_operation is a dynamic capability gateway: the MCP transport still requires a resource-bound bearer token, then the selected registered operation enforces its exact manifest feature scopes before confirmation, validation, or resolver execution. Missing scopes return an MCP tool error with an exact insufficient_scope WWW-Authenticate challenge so clients can request incremental authorization. Unknown IDs and extra variables are rejected, and no arbitrary document parameter exists.
rules.bounded-auth-first-request-body: MCP transport requests authenticate the resource-bound Bearer token before parsing JSON-RPC. Authenticated JSON request bodies are streamed with a 64 KiB limit, parsed once, and shared by scope enforcement, observability, rate limiting, and the SDK transport; larger bodies return 413 before tool handling.
rules.transport-observability: MCP transport handling emits production-safe structured request/response logs and Cloudflare Analytics Engine datapoints with JSON-RPC method summaries, tool names, argument keys, auth/origin phase, status, duration, and registered tool count; it never logs bearer tokens, cookies, or tool argument values.
rules.mutation-rate-limits: Ordinary MCP mutations are preflighted against the shared per-host, per-user feature-action Cloudflare budgets: 60 writes or 10 batch writes per minute. Registered GraphQL mutations instead use the shared resolver guard with the same feature:write key, avoiding duplicate consumption. Fixed-tool batches fail before side effects on limit or unavailable bindings; responses use 429 or 503 with Retry-After: 60.
rules.rate-limit-accuracy-boundary: MCP mutation throttles are per-Cloudflare-location, permissive, eventually consistent abuse controls rather than global exact quotas. The protocol does not promise remaining/reset counters, and read-only tools do not consume mutation budgets.
rules.flexible-date-inputs: Date and datetime parameters on MCP tools accept ISO 8601 with timezone offset (2026-05-01T08:00:00+08:00), bare date strings (2026-05-01, treated as UTC midnight for @db.Date columns), or timezone-less datetimes (2026-05-01T08:00:00, interpreted as Asia/Shanghai). Invalid strings produce a descriptive error response rather than a validation rejection.
rules.time-override: Time-sensitive tools (get_my_7days_timeline, get_upcoming_deadlines, get_my_overview, get_next_buses) accept an optional atTime parameter to anchor their internal clock to a caller-supplied moment instead of the server clock, enabling reproducible queries and future-scenario planning.
rules.openai-compatible-descriptors: Every registered tool advertises a title, a closed top-level object outputSchema with a required success boolean and typed known fields, MCP annotations for read-only/destructive/open-world hints, top-level securitySchemes, and _meta.securitySchemes. Ordinary tools declare fixed OAuth feature scopes; run_graphql_operation uses conservative static safety hints and delegates exact scopes to each manifest entry. Default and full results keep the same top-level key and collection types.
rules.single-tools-list-compatibility-wrapper: The SDK compatibility handler that hoists _meta.securitySchemes onto each tools/list descriptor is installed exactly once after all application tools are registered, rather than nesting one full-list wrapper per tool.
rules.tool-registry-completeness: Server startup fails when any registered MCP tool lacks an explicit OAuth scope entry or output schema, so tool registration cannot silently fall back to generic metadata.

notes: All tool calls are handled through this endpoint and require a Bearer token bound to the MCP protected resource /api/mcp; transport handling writes Cloudflare Analytics Engine datapoints for HTTP phase/status/duration, JSON-RPC method counts, and tool-call counts by tool name.
Trusted browser-origin Bearer-token clients receive CORS headers, including exposed MCP-Session-Id, Retry-After, and WWW-Authenticate response headers for browser transports.
Answers browser CORS preflight requests for the MCP transport headers used by POST/DELETE browser clients.
Requests with a foreign Origin header are rejected before MCP auth/tool handling.
Answers browser CORS preflight requests for trusted origins before bearer-token MCP transport handling.

rules.comment-write-scope: Ordinary signed-in user comment writes are available; admin moderation remains unavailable through MCP.
